model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: North Carolina holds a meaningful statistical edge over VCU, ranking 27th-29th nationally compared to VCU's 46th-51st, with advantages on both ends of the floor—particularly offensively (AdjO 121.5 vs 118.9) and defensively (AdjD 100.0 vs 103.0). However, the season-ending loss of star freshman Caleb Wilson, a projected top-5 NBA pick who was their frontcourt anchor, significantly narrows this gap and explains the razor-thin 2.5-point spread. VCU enters on a scorching 6-game winning streak having won 16 of their last 17, but their 1-5 record against NCAA Tournament-caliber teams exposes a ceiling that UNC's ACC-tested depth should still be able to exploit, even in a diminished state. The Tar Heels' remaining talent and roughly 4-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage should be enough to survive, though this is a legitimate upset threat on one of the tournament's most volatile seed lines.

reasoningStep.title: North Carolina vs VCU: 6/11 South Region First Round
reasoningStep.summary: UNC is the statistically superior team but enters significantly weakened by the season-ending loss of star freshman Caleb Wilson. VCU rides elite momentum but has struggled against tournament-caliber opponents. UNC's remaining depth and efficiency edge narrowly carry the day.
evidence: KenPom: UNC #29 (AdjEM +20.86, AdjO 121.5, AdjD 100.6) vs VCU #46 (AdjEM +16.96, AdjO 120.0, AdjD 103.0)
Torvik: UNC #27 vs VCU #51 with similar efficiency gaps
UNC star Caleb Wilson (projected top-5 NBA pick) out for season with broken right thumb requiring surgery
VCU on 6-game winning streak, 16-1 in last 17 games, but 1-5 vs NCAA Tournament teams
Spread: UNC -2.5, moneyline UNC -150 / VCU +125, reflecting a near coin-flip
6/11 seed line historically produces upsets approximately 37% of the time

result.text: VCU basketball: Location, March Madness history, more to know Skip to main content Home PGA Tour LPGA College Amateur Golf Life Equipment Apparel Newsletter Videos Events Odds Betting USA TODAY Sports Contact Us What to know about UNC basketball’s first-round March Madness opponent Rodd Baxley Fayetteville Observer UNC basketball is a No. 6 seed in the men’s 2026 NCAA Tournament and will face No. 11 seed VCU to tip off March Madness. The sixth-seeded Tar Heels (24-8) will take on the 11th-seeded Rams (27-7) on Thursday, March 19 in Greenville, S.C. The game tips off at 6:50 p.m. ET and will be televised on TNT . The winner will face No. 3 seed Illinois (24-8) or No. 14 seed Penn (18-11) in the round of 32 on Saturday, March 21. BRACKET CHALLENGE: Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today SURVIVOR POOL: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY’s Survivor Pool today! The Rams have taken down Duke and Kansas in NCAA Tournament games, but they’ll be playing UNC for the first time in program history. It's VCU's 21st March Madness appearance. Here are some things to know about VCU, what conference it competes in, its NCAA Tournament history and more: Where is VCU located? Location : Richmond, Virginia Student population : Virginia Commonwealth University has a student population of more than 29,000. Buy UNC NCAA Tournament tickets What conference is VCU in? The Rams have competed in the Atlantic 10 since 2012, winning the conference tournament in 2012, 2015, 2023, 2025 and 2026. Saint Louis is also representing the league in the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Who is VCU’s coach? Phil Martelli Jr., 44, is in his third year as a head coach, but it’s his debut season with the Rams. Martelli spent two seasons at Bryant, leading that program to the NCAA Tournament last season, before joining VCU. JD Barnett (1980), Anthony Grant (2007) and Will Wade (2016) were the other VCU coaches to lead the program to March Madness in their first seasons. Martelli Jr. is the son of longtime Saint Joseph’s coach Phil Martelli, who was with the Hawks for 34 years. VCU’s 2025-26 season rundown 2025-26 record : 27-7 (15-3 A-10) The Rams, who carry a six-game winning streak into the NCAA Tournament, have won 16 of their last 17 games. VCU won 10 in a row from mid-January to mid-February. It played six games against NCAA Tournament teams, posting a 1-5 record. But the Rams earned notable wins against South Florida and Virginia Tech in nonconference play. Watch NCAA Tournament games on Sling VCU is 15th nationally in 3-point shooting percentage, knocking down 36.7% of its shots from long range. Terrence Hill Jr., Jadrian Tracey and Nyk Lewis have attempted at least 125 shots from beyond the arc, with each of them making at least 35% of their 3-pointers. Hill, a lead guard, is VCU’s leading scorer and facilitator at 14.4 points and 2.8 assists per game, followed by 6-foot-11 center Lazar Djokovic at 13.5 points and 5.4 rebounds per game. result.text: UNC Basketball: Caleb Wilson injury leaves Tar Heels facing daunting ACC Tournament path Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports The harsh reality of Caleb Wilson's injury will start taking effect Wilson’s surgery removes North Carolina’s emotional spark and frontcourt anchor as the Tar Heels try to navigate the ACC Tournament and March Madness without him. Grant Chachere Tar Heels Wire North Carolina now has to face the sobering reality that Caleb Wilson will be on the bench instead of anchoring its frontcourt. As the Tar Heels finished their preparations Friday afternoon for their rematch with Duke, they received devastating news just before practice: Wilson had a broken right thumb that required surgery, effectively ending his freshman — and possibly only — season at Chapel Hill. The injury occurred the day before when he threw down a dunk during a 2-on-0 drill run by the Tar Heels at the start of practice. Wilson completed Thursday’s practice, but soreness the next morning led him to UNC athletic trainer Doug Halverson. X-rays showed a break and forced season-ending surgery before Friday’s practice. “There’s tremendous sadness for him. I’ve talked about how special — obviously he’s a special player, but just how remarkable of a kid and a teammate he is, and the passion that he has for his teammates, for North Carolina, for being on the floor, for playing in games like this,” Davis said. “It was a dream for him to play in the ACC and the NCAA Tournament, and my heart is broken that he won’t be able to do that.” While the injury news is shattering for UNC as it prepares to play Clemson on Thursday in the quarterfinals of the ACC Tournament, this isn’t uncharted territory for the Tar Heels. Including Wilson’s absence, North Carolina had to deal with Trimble’s nine-game absence during the nonconference schedule and Henri Veesaar’s two-game absence in February. That’s a total of 16 games without its top three scorers. In those games, Carolina was 13-3. North Carolina has gone 5–2 without Wilson, including wins over Clemson and Louisville — the ACC’s No. 5 and No. 6 seeds. It also showcases the how the Heels have dealt with adversity along with key players like Zayden High and Jarin Stevenson stepping up. Now the question remains: can UNC win a go to the Final Four, let alone win the ACC Tournament, without Wilson? It’s a question that everyone in Chapel Hill is wondering. However, it looks grim. Wilson was often the one who energized the team as its emotional spark, the player whose high-flying dunks brought the bench — and the crowd — to life. When Duke went on a 24-2 run in the second half of UNC’s 76-61 loss to the Blue Devils last Saturday, Wilson could only watch helplessly as his team struggled to find any rhythm. While North Carolina has a shot to make a run, it’s hard to beat Clemson after just knocking off the Tigers a week earlier. And even if the Tar Heels do

Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.

The sixth-seeded Tar Heels (24-8) will take on the 11th-seeded Rams (27-7) on Thursday, March 19 in Greenville, S.C. The game tips off at 6:50 p.m. ET and will be televised on TNT . The winner will face No. 3 seed Illinois (24-8) or No. 14 seed Penn (18-11) in the round of 32 on Saturday, March 21. BRACKET CHALLENGE: Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today SURVIVOR POOL: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY’s Survivor Pool today! The Rams have taken down Duke and Kansas in NCAA Tournament games, but they’ll be playing UNC for the first time in program history. It's VCU's 21st March Madness appearance. Here are some things to know about VCU, what conference it competes in, its NCAA Tournament history and more: Where is VCU located? Location : Richmond, Virginia Student population : Virginia Commonwealth University has a student population of more than 29,000. Buy UNC NCAA Tournament tickets What conference is VCU in? The Rams have competed in the Atlantic 10 since 2012, winning the conference tournament in 2012, 2015, 2023, 2025 and 2026. Saint Louis is also representing the league in the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Who is VCU’s coach? Phil Martelli Jr., 44, is in his third year as a head coach, but it’s his debut season with the Rams. Martelli spent two seasons at Bryant, leading that program to the NCAA Tournament last season, before joining VCU. JD Barnett (1980), Anthony Grant (2007) and Will Wade (2016) were the other VCU coaches to lead the program to March Madness in their first seasons. Martelli Jr. is the son of longtime Saint Joseph’s coach Phil Martelli, who was with the Hawks for 34 years. VCU’s 2025-26 season rundown 2025-26 record : 27-7 (15-3 A-10) The Rams, who carry a six-game winning streak into the NCAA Tournament, have won 16 of their last 17 games. VCU won 10 in a row from mid-January to mid-February. It played six games against NCAA Tournament teams, posting a 1-5 record. But the Rams earned notable wins against South Florida and Virginia Tech in nonconference play. Terrence Hill Jr., Jadrian Tracey and Nyk Lewis have attempted at least 125 shots from beyond the arc, with each of them making at least 35% of their 3-pointers. Hill, a lead guard, is VCU’s leading scorer and facilitator at 14.4 points and 2.8 assists per game, followed by 6-foot-11 center Lazar Djokovic at 13.5 points and 5.4 rebounds per game. result.text: UNC Basketball: Caleb Wilson injury leaves Tar Heels facing daunting ACC Tournament path Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports The harsh reality of Caleb Wilson's injury will start taking effect Wilson’s surgery removes North Carolina’s emotional spark and frontcourt anchor as the Tar Heels try to navigate the ACC Tournament and March Madness without him. Grant Chachere Tar Heels Wire North Carolina now has to face the sobering reality that Caleb Wilson will be on the bench instead of anchoring its frontcourt. As the Tar Heels finished their preparations Friday afternoon for their rematch with Duke, they received devastating news just before practice: Wilson had a broken right thumb that required surgery, effectively ending his freshman — and possibly only — season at Chapel Hill. The injury occurred the day before when he threw down a dunk during a 2-on-0 drill run by the Tar Heels at the start of practice. Wilson completed Thursday’s practice, but soreness the next morning led him to UNC athletic trainer Doug Halverson. X-rays showed a break and forced season-ending surgery before Friday’s practice. “There’s tremendous sadness for him. I’ve talked about how special — obviously he’s a special player, but just how remarkable of a kid and a teammate he is, and the passion that he has for his teammates, for North Carolina, for being on the floor, for playing in games like this,” Davis said. “It was a dream for him to play in the ACC and the NCAA Tournament, and my heart is broken that he won’t be able to do that.” While the injury news is shattering for UNC as it prepares to play Clemson on Thursday in the quarterfinals of the ACC Tournament, this isn’t uncharted territory for the Tar Heels. Including Wilson’s absence, North Carolina had to deal with Trimble’s nine-game absence during the nonconference schedule and Henri Veesaar’s two-game absence in February. That’s a total of 16 games without its top three scorers. In those games, Carolina was 13-3. North Carolina has gone 5–2 without Wilson, including wins over Clemson and Louisville — the ACC’s No. 5 and No. 6 seeds. It also showcases the how the Heels have dealt with adversity along with key players like Zayden High and Jarin Stevenson stepping up. Now the question remains: can UNC win a go to the Final Four, let alone win the ACC Tournament, without Wilson? It’s a question that everyone in Chapel Hill is wondering. However, it looks grim. Wilson was often the one who energized the team as its emotional spark, the player whose high-flying dunks brought the bench — and the crowd — to life. When Duke went on a 24-2 run in the second half of UNC’s 76-61 loss to the Blue Devils last Saturday, Wilson could only watch helplessly as his team struggled to find any rhythm. While North Carolina has a shot to make a run, it’s hard to beat Clemson after just knocking off the Tigers a week earlier. And even if the Tar Heels do

**Key findings:**

- **North Carolina (6-seed, 24-8):** KenPom #29, Torvik #27. AdjO 121.5 (32nd-33rd), AdjD 100.0-100.6 (34th). Adjusted efficiency margin ~+20.86. **Critical: Star freshman Caleb Wilson (projected top-5 NBA pick) is OUT for the season** with a broken right thumb requiring surgery. He had already missed time with a fractured left hand earlier. This is a massive loss — he was their frontcourt anchor and emotional spark.

- **VCU (11-seed, 27-7):** KenPom #46, Torvik #51. AdjO 118.9-120.0 (45th-49th), AdjD 103.0-103.8 (82nd). Adjusted efficiency margin ~+16.96.
